rationale: The citing paper explicitly positions itself as extending foreign-entry literature and includes capability constructs tied to prior country experience, while the cited paper is a foreign-entry study on knowledge effects in entry decisions—so a substantive conceptual link is plausible. However, the matched evidence is only a single DOI-level reference with no in-text citation context, so there is not enough direct evidence to confidently treat it as clearly substantive or clearly incidental.
